channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: Former WHO directors warn UK not to undermine vaping with new tobacco laws
item.description: Former WHO directors are warning the UK not to weaken one of its most effective smoking cessation tools. Vaping has played a major role in helping adult smokers move away from combustible tobacco, but new tobacco laws could reduce access, visibility and appeal for those who need it most. Protecting young people is essential, but policy should not make cigarettes the easier option. Evidence-based regulation should preserve vaping’s role in reducing smoking-related harm. (Published on June 22, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: Harm Reduction Policy Built Australia's Tobacco Black Market
item.description: Australia’s tobacco black market shows what happens when policy abandons harm reduction. High cigarette taxes, strict vaping rules and limited legal access to safer nicotine products have helped create space for illicit trade to grow. When smokers are priced out or blocked from lower-risk alternatives, demand does not disappear — it moves into unregulated markets. Better policy should reduce smoking-related harm without handing supply to criminals. (Published on June 22, 2026bywrdnews.org)
